author: @neon-pl3du
text: Great video ! Can I use Al foil instead of Grafoil ?

author: @davidrathbone6978
text: What you "won-a-do" is use some kitchen foil

replies.author: @nigeljohnson9820
replies.text: The lower resistance of aluminium foil suggests it would provide a better RF screen.

author: @NovaOnce
text: You just need to disable contactless payment in your bank. No more worries

replies.author: @RWBHere
replies.text: Unless the bank 'forgets' and re-enables it, without your knowledge. Then you're even more vulnerable, because you will not be expecting the security breach.

author: @nigeljohnson9820
text: How is Grafoil any better than using aluminium kitchen foil? What properties of Grafoil is superior to aluminium foil in this application?

replies.author: @RWBHere
replies.text:
It's a way for them to sell small quantities of grafoil to people, and to make a bit of profit to keep the lab research going. Grafoil is usually sold only in much larger quantities. Aluminium baking foil (or even chocolate bar wrapper) is cheaper, easy to buy, a better electrical conductor, easier to work with for this application, and 100% recyclable.

But, hey, this channel needs support, so why not think about making a contribution in some other way? 😉

author: @alexandracrawford800
text:
Whoever sets up the recording, so far in each case with this presenter, the volume is much lower than either R.M-S or other channel authors and difficult to hear without first adjusting the local output. Please attend to trying to standardize the  published volume? The overall quality of FWG videos has improved dramatically over the years. Yes, we all have to start somewhere.... thank you, 
RFID is typically much more susceptible to activation and interception than was originally intended so this has to be a real benefit - thinner than any bank statement!
